courses.name: Introduction to Programming
courses.description: Learn computational thinking and programming fundamentals using Python. Covers variables, control flow, functions, data structures, and basic algorithms. No prior programming experience required.
learningOutcomes: Write Python programs using variables, loops, and conditionals
Decompose problems into functions
Read and write data to files
Debug simple programs using error messages
Use lists, dictionaries, and sets appropriately

courses.name: Object-Oriented Programming
courses.description: Master object-oriented design principles using Java. Covers classes, inheritance, polymorphism, interfaces, generics, and design patterns.
learningOutcomes: Design class hierarchies with proper encapsulation
Apply SOLID principles to software design
Implement common design patterns (Singleton, Factory, Observer)
Write unit tests with JUnit
Build a multi-class application from requirements

courses.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
courses.description: Comprehensive study of fundamental data structures and algorithms. Analyze time/space complexity and implement solutions to classic computational problems.
learningOutcomes: Analyze algorithm complexity using Big-O notation
Implement and choose appropriate data structures (arrays, trees, graphs, hash tables)
Apply sorting, searching, and graph traversal algorithms
Solve dynamic programming problems
Prove algorithm correctness using invariants

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics for CS
courses.description: Mathematical foundations for computer science: logic, set theory, combinatorics, graph theory, and basic probability.
learningOutcomes: Construct formal proofs using mathematical induction
Model problems using graph theory
Apply combinatorics to counting problems
Use propositional and predicate logic for reasoning
Analyze recurrence relations

courses.name: Computer Architecture
courses.description: How computers work at the hardware-software interface: digital logic, CPU design, memory hierarchy, pipelining, and assembly language.
learningOutcomes: Write and debug assembly language programs
Explain CPU pipelining and superscalar execution
Design cache memory hierarchies
Understand virtual memory and TLB operation
Evaluate I/O and bus architectures

courses.name: Operating Systems
courses.description: Operating system design and implementation: processes, threads, synchronization, memory management, file systems, and virtualization.
learningOutcomes: Implement multi-threaded programs with proper synchronization
Explain process scheduling algorithms
Design memory allocation strategies
Implement a simple file system
Analyze OS security vulnerabilities

courses.name: Database Systems
courses.description: Relational database theory and practice: ER modeling, normalization, SQL, transactions, indexing, and NoSQL alternatives.
learningOutcomes: Design normalized relational schemas
Write complex SQL queries with joins and subqueries
Implement transaction processing with ACID guarantees
Choose appropriate indexing strategies
Compare relational and NoSQL databases for different use cases

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.description: TCP/IP protocol stack, routing, congestion control, application-layer protocols, network security, and emerging network architectures.
learningOutcomes: Explain the TCP/IP layered architecture
Implement a reliable transport protocol
Configure routing protocols (OSPF, BGP)
Analyze network security threats and defenses
Design RESTful APIs over HTTP

courses.name: Software Engineering
courses.description: Professional software development practices: requirements engineering, Agile/Scrum, version control, testing, CI/CD, and software architecture.
learningOutcomes: Write user stories and acceptance criteria
Use Git for collaborative version control
Design software using UML diagrams
Implement test-driven development (TDD)
Set up a CI/CD pipeline

courses.name: Web Development
courses.description: Full-stack web application development: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js, REST APIs, authentication, and deployment.
learningOutcomes: Build responsive web interfaces with HTML/CSS/JavaScript
Develop Single Page Applications with React
Create RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express
Implement user authentication with JWT
Deploy applications to cloud platforms

courses.name: Mobile App Development
courses.description: Native and cross-platform mobile development for iOS and Android. Covers UI design patterns, device APIs, app store deployment.
learningOutcomes: Build cross-platform apps with React Native or Flutter
Integrate device sensors (GPS, camera, accelerometer)
Manage app state and offline data
Publish apps to Apple App Store and Google Play
Design for accessibility and localization

courses.name: Cloud Computing
courses.description: Cloud-native application design on AWS/Azure/GCP: virtualization, containers, serverless, microservices, and infrastructure-as-code.
learningOutcomes: Deploy containerized applications with Docker and Kubernetes
Design serverless architectures with AWS Lambda
Implement auto-scaling and load balancing
Write infrastructure-as-code with Terraform
Monitor and optimize cloud costs

courses.name: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
courses.description: Principles of information security: cryptography, network security, application security, threat modeling, and incident response.
learningOutcomes: Apply symmetric and asymmetric encryption algorithms
Conduct basic penetration testing
Model threats using STRIDE methodology
Implement secure authentication and authorization
Respond to security incidents following NIST framework

courses.name: Machine Learning
courses.description: Supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms: regression, classification, clustering, ensemble methods, and model evaluation. Hands-on with scikit-learn and TensorFlow.
learningOutcomes: Implement linear and logistic regression from scratch
Apply decision trees, random forests, and gradient boosting
Build and train neural networks with TensorFlow/Keras
Evaluate models using cross-validation and appropriate metrics
Deploy ML models as REST APIs

courses.name: Deep Learning
courses.description: Advanced neural network architectures: CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, Transformers, GANs. Focus on computer vision, NLP, and generative AI applications.
learningOutcomes: Design and train convolutional neural networks for image tasks
Implement sequence models for text and time-series data
Build Transformer-based architectures from scratch
Fine-tune large pre-trained models (transfer learning)
Train generative adversarial networks

courses.name: Natural Language Processing
courses.description: Computational processing of human language: tokenization, embeddings, sequence labeling, parsing, machine translation, and large language models.
learningOutcomes: Implement word embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe)
Build named entity recognition and POS tagging systems
Design neural machine translation models
Fine-tune large language models for downstream tasks
Evaluate NLP systems with BLEU, ROUGE, and perplexity

courses.name: Computer Vision
courses.description: Image and video understanding: filtering, feature detection, object recognition, segmentation, 3D reconstruction, and video analysis.
learningOutcomes: Implement image filtering and edge detection
Build object detection systems (YOLO, R-CNN)
Design semantic segmentation models
Apply 3D reconstruction from stereo imagery
Deploy real-time video processing pipelines

courses.name: Introduction to Data Science
courses.description: Data science workflow: data wrangling, exploratory analysis, visualization, statistical inference, and communicating results. Heavy use of Python (pandas, matplotlib, seaborn).
learningOutcomes: Clean and transform messy real-world datasets
Conduct exploratory data analysis with appropriate visualizations
Apply statistical hypothesis testing
Build and interpret linear regression models
Communicate findings through data storytelling

courses.name: Big Data Technologies
courses.description: Distributed data processing: Hadoop, Spark, streaming systems, data lakes, and MLOps pipelines at scale.
learningOutcomes: Write MapReduce jobs and Spark transformations
Design data pipelines with Apache Kafka
Build ETL workflows for data warehouses
Implement MLOps practices (model versioning, monitoring)
Optimize Spark jobs for cluster performance

courses.name: Data Mining
courses.description: Discovering patterns in large datasets: association rules, clustering, anomaly detection, recommendation systems, and graph mining.
learningOutcomes: Implement Apriori and FP-Growth for association mining
Apply k-means, DBSCAN, and hierarchical clustering
Build collaborative filtering recommendation systems
Detect anomalies using statistical and ML methods
Mine graph data for community detection

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.description: Single-variable calculus: limits, derivatives, integrals, and the Fundamental Theorem. Applications to optimization and modeling.
learningOutcomes: Compute limits and derivatives of elementary functions
Apply differentiation to optimization problems
Evaluate definite and indefinite integrals
Solve differential equations for growth and decay models
Use Taylor series to approximate functions

courses.name: Calculus II
courses.description: Multi-variable calculus and vector analysis: partial derivatives, multiple integrals, line and surface integrals, and vector fields.
learningOutcomes: Compute partial derivatives and gradients
Evaluate double and triple integrals in various coordinate systems
Apply Green's, Stokes', and Divergence theorems
Optimize functions of several variables with constraints
Model physical systems using vector calculus

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.description: Vector spaces, matrices, linear transformations, eigenvalues, and applications in CS, data science, and engineering.
learningOutcomes: Solve systems of linear equations using matrix methods
Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Apply SVD and PCA for dimensionality reduction
Understand vector spaces and linear transformations abstractly
Use linear algebra in machine learning contexts

courses.name: Probability & Statistics
courses.description: Probability theory, random variables, distributions, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and Bayesian inference.
learningOutcomes: Compute probabilities using Bayes' theorem
Model random variables with common distributions
Construct confidence intervals and perform hypothesis tests
Apply Bayesian inference to update beliefs with data
Use R or Python for statistical computing

courses.name: Numerical Methods
courses.description: Numerical algorithms for solving mathematical problems on computers: root finding, interpolation, numerical integration, ODE solvers, and optimization.
learningOutcomes: Implement Newton-Raphson and bisection methods
Apply Gaussian quadrature for numerical integration
Solve ODEs with Runge-Kutta methods
Implement gradient descent variants for optimization
Analyze numerical stability and error propagation

courses.name: Principles of Management
courses.description: Foundational management concepts: planning, organizing, leading, controlling. Evolution of management theory and modern organizational practices.
learningOutcomes: Analyze organizational structures and design choices
Apply motivation theories to workplace scenarios
Develop strategic plans using SWOT analysis
Evaluate leadership styles for different contexts
Design performance management systems

courses.name: Financial Accounting
courses.description: The language of business: recording transactions, preparing financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow), and interpreting financial health.
learningOutcomes: Record business transactions using double-entry bookkeeping
Prepare and analyze financial statements
Apply GAAP/IFRS accounting standards
Calculate financial ratios for business analysis
Evaluate a company's financial health from its statements

courses.name: Microeconomics
courses.description: Individual and firm decision-making: supply and demand, market equilibrium, elasticity, production costs, market structures, and welfare economics.
learningOutcomes: Analyze market equilibrium shifts using supply and demand
Compute price elasticity of demand
Compare perfect competition, monopoly, and oligopoly outcomes
Evaluate policies using consumer and producer surplus
Apply game theory to strategic interactions

courses.name: Macroeconomics
courses.description: Economy-wide phenomena: GDP, inflation, unemployment, monetary and fiscal policy, international trade, and economic growth.
learningOutcomes: Calculate and interpret GDP and its components
Analyze inflation and unemployment tradeoffs
Evaluate monetary policy tools (interest rates, QE)
Model economic growth using Solow framework
Assess the impact of fiscal policy on aggregate demand

courses.name: Business Statistics
courses.description: Statistical methods for business decision-making: descriptive statistics, probability, sampling, regression analysis, and forecasting.
learningOutcomes: Summarize business data with descriptive statistics
Apply probability distributions to risk analysis
Build regression models for business forecasting
Design and analyze business surveys
Present statistical findings to non-technical stakeholders

courses.name: Corporate Finance
courses.description: Financial decision-making in corporations: capital budgeting, cost of capital, capital structure, dividend policy, and valuation.
learningOutcomes: Evaluate investment projects using NPV, IRR, and payback
Calculate weighted average cost of capital (WACC)
Analyze optimal capital structure (Modigliani-Miller)
Value companies using DCF and comparable multiples
Assess dividend and share repurchase policies

courses.name: Marketing Management
courses.description: Marketing strategy and execution: segmentation, targeting, positioning, the marketing mix (4Ps), brand building, and customer relationship management.
learningOutcomes: Segment markets and develop buyer personas
Design positioning strategies and value propositions
Develop integrated marketing communication plans
Analyze customer lifetime value (CLV)
Create go-to-market strategies for new products

courses.name: Organizational Behavior
courses.description: Human behavior in organizations: personality, perception, motivation, team dynamics, power, conflict, and organizational culture.
learningOutcomes: Analyze individual behavior using personality frameworks (Big Five, MBTI)
Design effective team structures and processes
Manage organizational conflict constructively
Assess and shape organizational culture
Develop inclusive leadership practices

courses.name: Operations Management
courses.description: Designing and managing production and service operations: process analysis, quality management, supply chain, lean, and Six Sigma.
learningOutcomes: Analyze process flows using Little's Law and queuing theory
Apply Six Sigma DMAIC methodology
Design lean production systems
Optimize inventory management (EOQ, safety stock)
Manage global supply chain risks

courses.name: Business Law
courses.description: Legal environment of business: contracts, torts, agency, business organizations, intellectual property, employment law, and international trade law.
learningOutcomes: Analyze contract formation and enforceability
Distinguish business organizational forms (LLC, Corp, Partnership)
Evaluate intellectual property protection strategies
Apply employment law to hiring and termination decisions
Assess legal risks in international business transactions

courses.name: Strategic Management
courses.description: Capstone: formulating and implementing business strategy. Industry analysis, competitive advantage, corporate strategy, and strategic change.
learningOutcomes: Conduct industry analysis using Porter's Five Forces
Identify sources of sustainable competitive advantage
Evaluate strategic options (cost leadership, differentiation, focus)
Design corporate diversification and M&A strategies
Develop strategy implementation and change management plans

courses.name: International Business
courses.description: Business in a global context: cross-cultural management, international trade theory, foreign market entry strategies, global supply chains, and exchange rate risk.
learningOutcomes: Analyze country attractiveness for market entry
Design market entry strategies (export, JV, FDI)
Manage cross-cultural teams across time zones
Hedge foreign exchange risk
Navigate international trade regulations and tariffs

courses.name: Entrepreneurship
courses.description: Creating and growing new ventures: opportunity recognition, business model design, lean startup methodology, fundraising, and scaling.
learningOutcomes: Identify and validate business opportunities
Design and test business models using Lean Canvas
Develop financial projections and pitch decks
Navigate venture capital and angel fundraising
Build minimum viable products (MVPs) and iterate

courses.name: Human Resource Management
courses.description: Strategic HR: talent acquisition, performance management, compensation, training & development, labor relations, and HR analytics.
learningOutcomes: Design competency-based recruitment and selection processes
Develop performance appraisal and feedback systems
Structure competitive compensation and benefits packages
Create learning and development programs
Apply HR analytics to workforce planning

courses.name: Supply Chain Management
courses.description: End-to-end supply chain: procurement, logistics, inventory, demand planning, supplier relationships, and sustainable supply chain practices.
learningOutcomes: Design supply chain networks for cost and resilience
Apply demand forecasting methods
Optimize inventory with EOQ and newsvendor models
Evaluate supplier performance and manage relationships
Implement sustainable and ethical sourcing practices

courses.name: Business Ethics
courses.description: Ethical decision-making in business: CSR, stakeholder theory, corporate governance, sustainability, and ethical leadership.
learningOutcomes: Apply ethical frameworks (utilitarian, deontological, virtue) to business dilemmas
Evaluate corporate social responsibility initiatives
Design ethical compliance programs
Analyze conflicts of interest and whistleblowing cases
Assess the ethics of AI and data use in business

courses.name: Digital Marketing
courses.description: Digital marketing channels and strategy: SEO, SEM, social media, content marketing, email marketing, analytics, and marketing automation.
learningOutcomes: Develop SEO strategies and optimize content for search engines
Design and manage Google Ads and social media campaigns
Build email marketing automation workflows
Analyze digital marketing metrics (CTR, CAC, ROAS, LTV)
Create data-driven marketing dashboards

courses.name: Investment Analysis
courses.description: Security analysis and portfolio management: equity valuation, fixed income, derivatives, modern portfolio theory, and behavioral finance.
learningOutcomes: Value equities using DCF, DDM, and relative valuation
Analyze fixed income securities (duration, convexity, yield curves)
Construct efficient portfolios using Markowitz optimization
Price options using Black-Scholes and binomial models
Identify behavioral biases affecting investment decisions

courses.name: Consumer Behavior
courses.description: Psychology of consumer decision-making: perception, motivation, attitudes, social influences, cultural factors, and behavioral economics in marketing.
learningOutcomes: Model consumer decision-making processes
Apply behavioral economics principles (nudging, framing, anchoring) to marketing
Analyze social and cultural influences on consumption
Design experiments to test consumer hypotheses
Segment consumers by psychographic and behavioral variables

courses.name: Leadership
courses.description: Leadership theory and practice: trait, behavioral, contingency, transformational, and servant leadership. Personal leadership development.
learningOutcomes: Assess personal leadership style using validated instruments
Apply situational leadership to team contexts
Develop emotional intelligence competencies
Design vision and change communication strategies
Build high-trust, high-performance team cultures

courses.name: Project Management
courses.description: Managing projects from initiation to closure: scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, stakeholder management. Aligned with PMBOK and Agile frameworks.
learningOutcomes: Develop project charters and scope statements
Build and critical-path-analyze project schedules (WBS, Gantt)
Estimate and manage project budgets
Identify and mitigate project risks
Tailor project methodology (Waterfall, Agile, Hybrid) to context

courses.name: Business Analytics
courses.description: Data-driven business decision-making: descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics using tools like Excel, SQL, Tableau, and Python.
learningOutcomes: Build interactive dashboards with Tableau or Power BI
Apply predictive models to business problems (churn, LTV, demand)
Use optimization models for resource allocation decisions
Communicate analytical insights to executives
Design A/B tests and analyze experimental results

courses.name: Negotiation & Conflict Resolution
courses.description: Theory and practice of negotiation: distributive and integrative bargaining, BATNA analysis, power dynamics, cross-cultural negotiation, and mediation.
learningOutcomes: Prepare negotiation strategies using BATNA and ZOPA frameworks
Apply integrative (win-win) bargaining techniques
Manage power asymmetries in negotiations
Navigate cross-cultural negotiation differences
Mediate disputes as a neutral third party

courses.name: E-Commerce
courses.description: Digital commerce: platform business models, payment systems, conversion optimization, marketplace dynamics, and e-commerce analytics.
learningOutcomes: Design e-commerce platform strategies (B2C, B2B, C2C, D2C)
Optimize conversion funnels and checkout flows
Integrate payment gateways and manage fraud risk
Analyze marketplace network effects
Apply SEO and CRO to e-commerce sites

courses.name: FinTech
courses.description: Technology-driven financial innovation: blockchain, cryptocurrencies, robo-advisory, P2P lending, crowdfunding, and regulatory technology (RegTech).
learningOutcomes: Explain blockchain consensus mechanisms and smart contracts
Evaluate DeFi protocols and their risks
Design robo-advisory algorithms for portfolio management
Analyze regulatory frameworks for FinTech (KYC, AML, PSD2)
Assess the disruption potential of embedded finance

courses.name: Advanced Algorithms
courses.description: Graduate-level algorithm design: network flows, linear programming, approximation algorithms, randomized algorithms, and parameterized complexity.
learningOutcomes: Design approximation algorithms for NP-hard problems
Apply linear programming duality to optimization
Implement randomized algorithms with provable guarantees
Analyze online algorithms with competitive ratios
Prove lower bounds for algorithmic problems

courses.name: Distributed Systems
courses.description: Design and implementation of distributed systems: consensus, replication, consistency models, distributed transactions, and fault tolerance.
learningOutcomes: Implement consensus algorithms (Paxos, Raft)
Design eventually-consistent distributed databases
Handle network partitions with CAP theorem awareness
Build fault-tolerant systems with replication
Analyze distributed system performance bottlenecks

courses.name: AI Ethics & Society
courses.description: Ethical implications of AI: fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, bias, alignment, and the societal impact of automation.
learningOutcomes: Audit ML models for fairness using demographic parity and equalized odds
Apply explainability techniques (LIME, SHAP)
Design AI governance frameworks for organizations
Evaluate the societal impact of autonomous systems
Develop ethical guidelines for responsible AI deployment

courses.name: Managerial Accounting
courses.description: Using accounting information for internal decision-making: cost behavior, CVP analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, and performance measurement.
learningOutcomes: Analyze cost behavior (fixed, variable, mixed)
Apply CVP analysis for break-even and target profit planning
Design master budgets and flexible budgets
Compute and interpret cost and revenue variances
Design balanced scorecard performance systems

courses.name: Financial Management
courses.description: Advanced corporate financial strategy: capital structure theory, M&A, corporate restructuring, risk management, and value-based management.
learningOutcomes: Value companies for M&A using DCF and transaction comparables
Structure leveraged buyouts and analyze LBO models
Design corporate hedging strategies with derivatives
Apply real options analysis to strategic investments
Develop value-based management systems (EVA, shareholder value)

courses.name: Marketing Strategy
courses.description: Strategic marketing at the C-suite level: brand equity, pricing strategy, channel strategy, customer experience design, and marketing ROI.
learningOutcomes: Measure and manage brand equity
Design value-based pricing strategies
Optimize omni-channel distribution
Calculate marketing ROI and customer acquisition cost
Develop customer experience (CX) strategies

courses.name: Research Methods in Business
courses.description: Doctoral-level research design: qualitative, quantitative, and mixed methods. Literature review, hypothesis development, measurement, and research ethics.
learningOutcomes: Design rigorous research studies (experimental, quasi-experimental, case study)
Develop testable hypotheses from theory
Construct and validate measurement instruments
Apply advanced statistical methods (SEM, HLM, panel data)
Write research proposals aligned with journal standards

courses.name: Strategic Leadership & Governance
courses.description: Executive-level strategic leadership: board governance, stakeholder management, corporate political activity, strategic change, and CEO succession.
learningOutcomes: Analyze board composition and governance effectiveness
Design stakeholder engagement strategies
Evaluate strategic change initiatives through case analysis
Develop CEO succession planning frameworks
Apply upper echelons theory to strategic decisions

courses.name: Theory of Computation
courses.description: Formal models of computation: finite automata, pushdown automata, Turing machines, computability, and complexity classes (P, NP, PSPACE).
learningOutcomes: Prove language regularity/non-regularity using pumping lemma
Construct Turing machines for computational problems
Classify problems as decidable, recognizable, or undecidable
Prove NP-completeness via polynomial-time reduction
Explain the P vs NP problem and its implications

courses.name: Compiler Design
courses.description: Building compilers: lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate representation, code optimization, and code generation.
learningOutcomes: Implement a lexer using regular expressions and finite automata
Build recursive-descent and LR parsers for context-free grammars
Design intermediate representations (AST, TAC, SSA)
Apply dataflow analysis for code optimization
Generate target machine code from IR

courses.name: Human-Computer Interaction
courses.description: User-centered design: usability principles, interaction design, prototyping, evaluation methods, accessibility, and emerging interaction paradigms.
learningOutcomes: Conduct user research (interviews, surveys, observation)
Create wireframes, prototypes, and interaction flows
Apply usability heuristics and conduct heuristic evaluations
Design and run usability tests with quantitative metrics
Implement accessible interfaces following WCAG guidelines

courses.name: Computer Graphics
courses.description: Real-time rendering: rasterization, shading, ray tracing, geometric modeling, animation, and GPU programming with OpenGL/WebGL.
learningOutcomes: Implement the 3D rendering pipeline (model, view, projection transforms)
Write vertex and fragment shaders in GLSL
Apply Phong and PBR shading models
Implement shadow mapping and environment mapping
Build a simple ray tracer from scratch

courses.name: Computer Science Capstone
courses.description: Team-based capstone project: full software development lifecycle from requirements to deployment. Work with external stakeholders on a real-world problem.
learningOutcomes: Deliver a complete software product from concept to deployment
Collaborate effectively in an Agile team
Present technical work to non-technical stakeholders
Manage project scope, timeline, and risks
Document system architecture and user guides

courses.name: MCS Thesis / Capstone
courses.description: Master's thesis or applied project: original research or significant software system development under faculty supervision.
learningOutcomes: Formulate a research question or engineering problem
Conduct a systematic literature review
Design and execute a rigorous methodology
Present findings in a formal thesis defense
Produce publishable-quality research or production-ready software

courses.name: MBA Capstone / Consulting Project
courses.description: Applied strategic consulting project for a real organization. Teams diagnose problems, analyze data, and present actionable recommendations to C-suite.
learningOutcomes: Diagnose complex organizational problems
Collect and analyze business data from multiple sources
Develop strategic recommendations with implementation roadmaps
Present findings to executive leadership
Manage client relationships and expectations

courses.name: DBA Dissertation
courses.description: Original doctoral research contributing to business theory and practice. Independent research culminating in a dissertation defense.
learningOutcomes: Identify a significant gap in business literature
Design and execute original empirical research
Contribute novel theoretical insights to the field
Defend dissertation before an academic committee
Publish findings in peer-reviewed journals

courses.name: Design & Analysis of Algorithms
courses.description: Advanced techniques for algorithm design and analysis: divide-and-conquer, dynamic programming, amortized analysis, randomization, network flow, linear programming, and approximation algorithms. Modeled after MIT 6.046.
learningOutcomes: Analyze algorithm complexity using advanced asymptotic techniques
Apply network flow and linear programming to optimization problems
Design approximation algorithms for NP-hard problems
Implement randomized algorithms with provable guarantees
Prove correctness and optimality of greedy algorithms

courses.name: Computer System Engineering
courses.description: Principles of computer system design: modularity, abstraction, naming, networking, fault tolerance, security, and real-world case studies including the Internet, DNS, and cloud systems. Modeled after MIT 6.033.
learningOutcomes: Design modular systems with clean abstractions
Analyze system trade-offs in performance, reliability, and security
Apply fault-tolerance techniques including replication and consensus
Evaluate real-world systems through case-study methodology
Design naming and addressing schemes for distributed systems

courses.name: Distributed Systems Engineering
courses.description: Engineering distributed systems: RPC, threads, concurrency control, replication, consensus (Raft/Paxos), fault tolerance, and distributed transactions. Heavy implementation focus — students build a key-value store. Modeled after MIT 6.824.
learningOutcomes: Implement the Raft consensus algorithm
Design linearizable distributed storage systems
Apply two-phase commit for distributed transactions
Debug distributed race conditions and deadlocks
Evaluate consistency vs availability trade-offs (CAP)

courses.name: Computer Systems Security
courses.description: Security architecture and engineering: threat modeling, privilege separation, capability systems, information flow control, sandboxing, and web security. Includes hands-on security lab projects. Modeled after MIT 6.858.
learningOutcomes: Conduct systematic threat modeling using STRIDE and attack trees
Design privilege-separated system architectures
Implement information flow control mechanisms
Analyze web application vulnerabilities (XSS, CSRF, SQLi)
Build secure sandbox environments

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Science
courses.description: Harvard's legendary introduction to the intellectual enterprises of computer science and the art of programming. Covers C, Python, SQL, HTML/CSS/JavaScript, algorithms, data structures, and computational thinking. Modeled after Harvard CS50.
learningOutcomes: Write programs in C, Python, and JavaScript
Design and query SQL databases
Implement fundamental algorithms and data structures
Build full-stack web applications
Think algorithmically and solve problems efficiently

courses.name: Machine Learning
courses.description: A comprehensive introduction to machine learning: supervised and unsupervised learning, Bayesian methods, kernel methods, neural networks, and reinforcement learning. Modeled after Harvard CS181.
learningOutcomes: Derive and implement Bayesian and frequentist ML algorithms
Apply kernel methods including SVMs and Gaussian processes
Design and train deep neural networks for classification and regression
Evaluate bias-variance trade-offs in model selection
Implement reinforcement learning algorithms

courses.name: Artificial Intelligence: Principles & Techniques
courses.description: Survey of modern AI: search, Markov decision processes, game playing, constraint satisfaction, Bayesian networks, and logic. Emphasis on practical implementation. Modeled after Stanford CS221.
learningOutcomes: Implement A* search and adversarial search algorithms
Model sequential decision problems as MDPs
Design Bayesian networks for probabilistic reasoning
Apply constraint satisfaction for scheduling and planning
Build game-playing agents using minimax and Monte Carlo tree search

courses.name: Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning
courses.description: Cutting-edge NLP with deep learning: word vectors, RNNs, LSTMs, attention, Transformers, BERT, GPT architectures, and large language models. Modeled after Stanford CS224N.
learningOutcomes: Implement word2vec and GloVe embeddings
Design sequence-to-sequence models with attention
Build and fine-tune Transformer architectures
Pre-train and adapt large language models
Evaluate NLP systems on benchmarks (GLUE, SuperGLUE)

courses.name: Reinforcement Learning
courses.description: Theoretical and practical reinforcement learning: MDPs, value iteration, policy gradients, Q-learning, deep RL, exploration, and applications to robotics and games. Modeled after Stanford CS234.
learningOutcomes: Implement value iteration and policy iteration algorithms
Apply deep Q-networks (DQN) to complex environments
Design policy gradient methods (REINFORCE, PPO)
Balance exploration vs exploitation in online learning
Evaluate RL agents in simulated environments (Gym)

courses.name: Introduction to Data Science
courses.description: The data science lifecycle: data wrangling, exploratory analysis, statistical modeling, machine learning, and communication of results. Heavy use of R and Python. Modeled after Harvard Data Science course.
learningOutcomes: Wrangle messy real-world datasets into analysis-ready form
Perform exploratory data analysis with ggplot2 and matplotlib
Fit and interpret regression and classification models
Communicate data insights through visualizations and reports
Apply the full data science workflow to a capstone project

courses.name: Statistical Learning
courses.description: Modern statistical learning methods: regularization, splines, GAMs, trees, random forests, boosting, SVMs, and deep learning. Emphasis on statistical foundations. Modeled after Stanford Stats 216 / ISLR.
learningOutcomes: Apply ridge regression and LASSO for high-dimensional data
Build tree-based ensembles (random forest, gradient boosting)
Analyze the bias-variance trade-off in model selection
Implement cross-validation and bootstrap for model assessment
Interpret black-box models using SHAP and LIME

courses.name: Introduction to Psychology
courses.description: A comprehensive survey of psychological science: brain and behavior, sensation and perception, learning and memory, cognition, development, social psychology, personality, and psychopathology. Modeled after Harvard's introductory psychology course.
learningOutcomes: Describe major theoretical perspectives in psychology
Explain the neural basis of behavior and cognition
Evaluate research methods and experimental designs
Apply psychological principles to everyday situations
Critically analyze claims about human behavior using evidence

courses.name: Social Psychology
courses.description: How social situations shape human behavior: conformity, obedience, persuasion, group dynamics, prejudice, attraction, and altruism. Classic experiments and modern replications. Modeled after Stanford social psychology.
learningOutcomes: Analyze classic social psychology experiments (Milgram, Asch, Zimbardo)
Apply attribution theory to explain social judgments
Design experiments to test social influence hypotheses
Evaluate the replication crisis and open science practices
Develop interventions to reduce prejudice and discrimination

courses.name: Cognitive Science
courses.description: The interdisciplinary study of mind and intelligence: perception, attention, memory, language, reasoning, decision-making, and consciousness. Integrates psychology, neuroscience, computer science, and philosophy. Modeled after MIT 9.012.
learningOutcomes: Model cognitive processes using computational frameworks
Analyze perceptual illusions to understand sensory processing
Compare theories of memory encoding, storage, and retrieval
Evaluate the modularity of mind hypothesis
Apply Bayesian models to understand human reasoning

courses.name: Microeconomic Theory & Public Policy
courses.description: Advanced microeconomic theory: consumer and producer theory, general equilibrium, welfare economics, market failures, game theory, and mechanism design. Applications to public policy. Modeled after MIT 14.121.
learningOutcomes: Derive demand functions from utility maximization
Analyze general equilibrium in competitive markets
Design mechanisms for efficient resource allocation
Apply game theory to strategic interactions and auctions
Evaluate public policies using welfare economics frameworks

courses.name: Behavioral Economics
courses.description: How psychological factors influence economic decision-making: heuristics, biases, framing, prospect theory, nudging, and time inconsistency. Policy applications. Modeled after Harvard's behavioral economics course.
learningOutcomes: Identify cognitive biases (anchoring, availability, representativeness)
Apply prospect theory to decisions under risk
Design nudges and choice architecture for policy
Analyze intertemporal choice and self-control problems
Evaluate the ethics of behavioral interventions

courses.name: Econometrics: Causal Inference
courses.description: Modern causal inference methods: randomized experiments, matching, difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, and synthetic control. Heavy use of R and Stata. Modeled after Stanford econometrics.
learningOutcomes: Design and analyze randomized controlled experiments
Apply difference-in-differences for policy evaluation
Implement instrumental variables for endogenous regressors
Use regression discontinuity for quasi-experimental designs
Critique causal claims in published economic research

courses.name: Negotiation & Deal-Making
courses.description: The art and science of negotiation: distributive and integrative bargaining, BATNA analysis, coalition dynamics, cross-cultural negotiation, and deal design. Case-method teaching. Modeled after Harvard Business School's legendary negotiation course.
learningOutcomes: Prepare and execute multi-issue negotiation strategies
Analyze BATNA and reservation points systematically
Build and sustain coalitions in multi-party negotiations
Navigate cross-cultural negotiation differences
Structure complex deal terms for mutual gain

courses.name: Entrepreneurship & Venture Creation
courses.description: From idea to venture: opportunity recognition, customer discovery, lean startup methodology, business model design, fundraising, and scaling. Students develop and pitch their own venture. Modeled after Stanford GSB entrepreneurship course.
learningOutcomes: Identify and validate entrepreneurial opportunities
Conduct customer discovery interviews (minimum 20)
Design and iterate business models using Lean Canvas
Build and present compelling pitch decks to investors
Navigate venture capital term sheets and cap tables

courses.name: Automata, Computability & Complexity
courses.description: MIT 6.045: Turing machines, decidability, P vs NP, polynomial-time reductions, limits of computation.
learningOutcomes: Prove undecidability via reduction
Classify P, NP, PSPACE problems
Construct polynomial-time reductions
Explain Cook-Levin theorem

courses.name: Mining Massive Datasets
courses.description: Stanford CS246: MapReduce, PageRank, recommender systems, social network analysis, streaming algorithms.
learningOutcomes: Implement MapReduce algorithms
Build recommendation systems
Analyze social network graphs
Apply streaming algorithms

courses.name: Introduction to Neuroscience
courses.description: Stanford: Neural signaling, sensory systems, motor control, learning and memory, brain development.
learningOutcomes: Explain action potential mechanisms
Map sensory brain pathways
Analyze synaptic learning
Design fMRI paradigms

courses.name: Human Perception
courses.description: Oxford: Vision, audition, touch, taste, multisensory integration. Psychophysics, signal detection theory.
learningOutcomes: Apply signal detection theory
Design psychophysical experiments
Model perceptual decision-making
Analyze multisensory integration

courses.name: Game Theory & Economic Applications
courses.description: Stanford ECON 280: Nash equilibrium, subgame perfection, Bayesian games, mechanism design, auctions, evolutionary game theory.
learningOutcomes: Compute Nash equilibria
Design incentive-compatible mechanisms
Analyze auction strategies
Apply evolutionary stability

courses.name: Labour Economics
courses.description: Oxford: Wage determination, human capital, discrimination, migration, unions, labor market policy.
learningOutcomes: Analyze education returns via Mincer
Evaluate minimum wage effects
Model migration decisions
Assess labor market discrimination

courses.name: Design Thinking & Innovation
courses.description: Stanford d.school: Human-centered design — empathy, ideation, prototyping, testing, iteration. Applied to real-world challenges.
learningOutcomes: Conduct empathy interviews
Facilitate ideation sessions
Build and test rapid prototypes
Apply design thinking to strategy

courses.name: Corporate Governance & Boards
courses.description: HBS: Board structure, director duties, shareholder activism, executive compensation, governance across legal systems.
learningOutcomes: Evaluate board composition
Design executive compensation
Analyze shareholder activism
Compare governance jurisdictions

courses.name: Causal Inference
courses.description: MIT: Potential outcomes, DAGs, instrumental variables, difference-in-differences, RDD, and modern causal ML methods.
learningOutcomes: Apply potential outcomes framework
Design instrumental variable studies
Implement difference-in-differences
Use causal graphical models
